steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
At an intersection, the two expressions for $y$ are equal.
$$2x^2-25x+84=3x+a$$
$$2x^2-28x+(84-a)=0$$

steps.title: Use one intersection
steps.content:
The graphs intersect at exactly one point, so the quadratic equation has exactly one solution. That solution is the vertex of the quadratic in $x$. For $2x^2-28x+(84-a)=0$, the vertex $x$-value is:
$$x=\frac{-(-28)}{2(2)}=\frac{28}{4}=7$$

steps.title: Match the x-value
steps.content:
The single intersection has $x=7$.
The answer is C.
